mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate reflects labor market tightness and robust economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days in any macro environment.
failure_condition: If rolling 2-year median 20-day return when UNRATE is below 5% is not positive or if the Rank IC between UNRATE level and forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation erodes real purchasing power and raises the risk premium demanded by equity investors, causing forward returns to be negative when consumer prices rise at an annualized rate above 2.5% over three months.
failure_condition: If triggered instances show positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate broad-based economic expansion that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint upward trend in HOUST and INDPRO fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong year-over-year growth in retail sales signals robust consumer spending, a key driver of corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.5% signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS above 0.5% predicts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with growing retail sales reflects a virtuous cycle of income growth and consumer demand that boosts corporate profits and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If the joint positive trend in PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is very low (below 4%) and begins to rise over three months, it signals late-cycle deterioration in the labor market that precedes negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4% and 3-month change above 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: If returns in triggered windows are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) relative to baseline over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A strong year-over-year increase in industrial production during the post-recession recovery period signals a durable manufacturing expansion that positively impacts forward equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If triggered periods in post-recession environments do not produce significantly positive 60-day returns (z-test p > 0.05) compared to all other periods.
